chief_complaint: A 70-year-old woman presented with decreased vision in the right eye.
history: The patient has a history of smoking and presented with reduced vision in the right eye due to CRVO. She had a productive morning cough with white sputum, shortness of breath, involuntary weight loss, morning headaches, and fatigue over the past 3 months. She had well-controlled hyperlipidemia but lacked classic CRVO risk factors.
exam: Clinical ophthalmological examination, multimodal imaging, and physical examination revealed findings consistent with CRVO. Pulmonary examination, spirometry, and thorax imaging showed evidence of COPD.
labs: Elevated polyclonal free light chains (FLCs), elevated Beta-2 microglobulin (β2M), and anemia were noted. Other investigations were consistent with CRVO.
correct_diagnosis: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) as a potential underlying condition in Central Retinal Vein Occlusion (CRVO)
differentials: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) as a potential underlying condition in Central Retinal Vein Occlusion (CRVO)
correct_ranking: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) as a potential underlying condition in Central Retinal Vein Occlusion (CRVO)
